2605.30117.title: VLA-Trace: Diagnosing Vision-Language-Action Models through Representation and Behavior Tracing
2605.30117.paper_summary: Understanding how Vision-Language-Action (VLA) models transform multimodal knowledge into embodied control remains an open challenge. We present VLA-Trace, a progressive diagnostic framework that analyzes VLA models through a unified evidence chain from representation dynamics to causal control attribution and behavioral manifestation. It specifically combines cross-modal and checkpoint-drift centered kernel alignment (CKA) to trace representation evolution, attention knockout interventions to identify modality-specific control pathways, and rollout-level behavioral probes to examine grounding, shortcut dependence, and semantic following. Experiments on $π_{0.5}$ and OpenVLA reveal three key findings. First, the two models exhibit distinct modality-specific adaptation dynamics during VLA finetuning. Second, they rely on different multimodal routing strategies and layer-wise dependencies during action decoding. Third, although VLA policies excel at visually grounded trajectory generation, they remain limited in fine-grained semantic following. These findings highlight future directions for representation-preserving adaptation, causal VLA circuits, and compositional semantic control.
2605.30117.paper_summary_zh: 理解視覺-語言-行動 (VLA) 模型如何將多模態知識轉化為具身控制仍然是一個未解的挑戰。<lb/>我們提出了 VLA-Trace，一個進階的診斷框架，通過從表徵動態到因果控制歸因及行為表現的統一證據鏈來分析 VLA 模型。<lb/>它特別結合了跨模態和檢查點漂移中心的核對齊 (CKA) 來追蹤表徵演變，注意力干預來識別模態特定的控制路徑，以及回滾級別的行為探針來檢查基礎、捷徑依賴和語義跟隨。<lb/>在 $π_{0.5}$ 和 OpenVLA 上的實驗揭示了三個關鍵發現。<lb/>首先，這兩個模型在 VLA 微調期間展現出不同的模態特定適應動態。<lb/>其次，它們在行動解碼過程中依賴不同的多模態路由策略和層級依賴。<lb/>第三，儘管 VLA 策略在視覺基礎的軌跡生成方面表現優異，但在細粒度語義跟隨方面仍然有限。<lb/>這些發現突顯了未來在保留表徵的適應、因果 VLA 電路和組合語義控制方面的研究方向。
2605.30117.author: Haoyuan Shi et.al.
2605.30117.authors: Haoyuan Shi, Xiancong Ren, Yingji Zhang, Qinfan Zhang, Jiayu Hu, Haozhe Shan, Han Dong, Jinpeng Lu, Yinda Chen, Yi Zhang, Yong Dai, Xiaozhu Ju
